question: Which quarter shows the best overall performance based on the stacked bar chart?

options.A: Q1 shows the best performance with the lowest electricity consumption and few customer comments.
options.B: Q2 shows the best performance because it has the highest combined bar height.
options.C: Q4 shows the best performance with higher store visits than Q1 and Q3.
options.D: The chart cannot determine overall performance accurately because it stacks different metrics with incompatible units.

explanation: The chart stacks unrelated metrics (Store Visits, Electricity Consumption, etc.) into a single total. These metrics have different units and meanings (e.g., counts, temperatures, consumption), so adding them together creates a meaningless sum. The stacked height cannot be used to assess overall performance. Only individual metric comparisons are valid.
